rulings.Case Title: PRIMO ANGELI VS. THEODORE ROOSEVELT ASSOCIATION, A NONPROFIT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint And / Or Stay Or Dismiss Action; Declarations Of Tweed Roosvelt And Howard Ehrlich In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 8, 2010, line 5. DEFENDANT THEODORE ROOSEVELT ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint And/Or Stay Or Dismiss Action. Grant motion to quash service of summons and complaint. Grant motion to dismiss. Plaintiff has failed to establish that defendant has sufficient contacts to justify general or specific jurisdiction. Defendant TRA does not have continuous and systematic contacts within Ca., did not purposefully avail itself of forum benefits, and the claim does not arise out of TRA's contacts with Ca. TRA is based in Oyster Bay, New York. It has no offices or chapters in Ca. and does not engage in activities there. TRA allegedly engaged plaintiff to do design work relating to logos and branding for a museum in the planning stages in Oyster Bay, New York. Plaintiff is an individual who is a resident of Italy, comes to San Francisco occasionally on business, and maintains an office in San Francisco. Plaintiff negotiated and formed the alleged contract that is the subject of this dispute in Italy. (Angeli Decl., ex. B, referring to "the computer and my new office now functional, finally moved into our new residence and the Italian government not changed that much since the days of Benito Mussolini.") Although Plaintiff states that his email two days later "accepting" defendant's offer to perform design services was allegedly "sent" from plaintiff's San Francisco office, plaintiff does not state that he was in Ca. when the contract was formed. No meetings between the parties took place in Ca.; Plaintiff made a presentation to TRA in Oyster Bay, New York; other meetings with representatives of TRA occurred in Florida, Georgia and New York City. The work was to be done for a project in Oyster Bay; there were no contemplated future consequences for Ca. The work was allegedly completed between approximately Jan. 2008 and Oct. 2008 (Angeli Decl. paras. 8 and 13). Plaintiff's statement that "all of the work was performed

rulings.Case Title: MARY LUCAS VS. RECOMMIND, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT

First affirmative defense: deny. Second AD: deny. Third AD: deny. Fourth AD: deny. Fifth AD: grant with leave to amend to plead with more specificity. Sixth AD: deny. Tenth AD: deny. 11th AD: deny. 16th AD: deny. 17th AD: deny. 18th AD: deny. 19th AD: grant. =(302/MJM) 4:34 pm - Clerk called counsel to advise the tentative ruling is changed as to the 4th AD. Motion granted as to 4th AD.

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BY AND THROUGH, VS. CARL ERNST JR
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 8, 2010, line 12. This is a late tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CARL ERNST DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer based on failure to comply with CCP 1166(a)(5) is overruled. Defendant complied with the statute by incorporating the 3-day notice in the 2nd Amended Complaint (Ex. 2); the top of the notice states the mode of service. The demurrer based on uncertainty is sustained in part only with respect to the first ground for breach alleged in the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") at paragraph 8 (rejection of lease), with leave to amend to plead with more specificity the actual lease provision that was allegedly breached when Pier 38 Maritime Recreation Center, Inc., through its Bankruptcy Trustee, rejected the lease effective August 8, 2009. CCP 430.10(f). As to the remainder of the SAC, the demurrer based on uncertainty is overruled. As to the second ground of breach alleged in the SAC at paragraph 8 (that Defendants' conveyed, subleased or transferred an interest in the Lease to third parties, as therein alleged), the demurrer for failure to state a claim sufficient to state an action for Unlawful Detainer or any other cause of action is denied. (CCP 430.10(e)) The Court cannot resolve the interpretation of the lease issues by this demurrer. The demurrer as to uncertainty (CCP 430.10(f)) for this second ground of breach is also overruled. =(302/MJM)
